source_docs.title: A human cell atlas of fetal chromatin accessibility

oligos.name: Tn5 mosaic-end adapter A (Nextera Read 1 / i5 transposon strand)
oligos.role: Transposon adapter (transferred strand) loaded on standard Nextera Tn5; deposited on one end of every tagmented fragment and provides the Read 1 sequencing primer site
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TDE1 (Nextera) loading. 3' 19 nt (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG) is the mosaic end; identical to the 3Lv2_R1_seq sequencing primer.

oligos.name: Tn5 mosaic-end adapter B (Nextera Read 2 / i7 transposon strand)
oligos.role: Transposon adapter (transferred strand) loaded on standard Nextera Tn5; deposited on the other end of every tagmented fragment and provides the Read 2 sequencing primer site
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TDE1 (Nextera) loading. Identical to the 3Lv2_R2_seq sequencing primer.

oligos.name: Tn5 mosaic-end reverse (non-transferred, phosphorylated) strand
oligos.role: 5'-phosphorylated non-transferred strand of the Tn5 transposon; anneals to the mosaic end of both ME-A and ME-B
oligos.notes: Carries a 5' phosphate. Standard Nextera ME-rev common to both loaded adapters.

oligos.name: 3LV2_N5_Splint
oligos.role: Bridging splint oligo that anneals across the N5-oligo/ME-A junction to template the first (N5) ligation; 3' blocked so it is not itself ligated/extended
oligos.notes: Synthesized as GCCGACGACTGATTA/3ddC/ (3' dideoxy-C block). Reverse-complementary to TAATCAGTCGTCGGC, spanning the N5-oligo 3' end (...GTAATCAG) and the ME-A 5' end (TCGTCGGC...).

oligos.name: 3LV2_N7_Splint
oligos.role: Bridging splint oligo that anneals across the N7-oligo/ME-B junction to template the second (N7) ligation; 3' blocked
oligos.notes: Synthesized as CACGAGACGACAAGT/3ddC/. Reverse-complementary to ACTTGTCGTCTCGTG, spanning the N7-oligo 3' end (...GACTTGTC) and the ME-B 5' end (GTCTCGTG...).

oligos.name: 3LV2_N5_Oligo (384 barcodes, round-1 index)
oligos.role: First-round (N5) ligation barcode oligo; splint-ligated onto the ME-A end. 3' constant GTAATCAG ligates to ME-A; 5' constant CACCGCACGAGAGGT is the P5-PCR priming site
oligos.notes: 384 distinct barcodes (Plates 1-4, A01-H12). Example A01_Plate1: CACCGCACGAGAGGTAAGTAGACTAGTAATCAG (barcode AAGTAGACTA).

oligos.name: 3LV2_N7_Oligo (384 barcodes, round-2 index)
oligos.role: Second-round (N7) ligation barcode oligo; splint-ligated onto the ME-B end. 3' constant GACTTGTC ligates to ME-B; 5' constant CAGCACGGCGAGACT is the P7-PCR priming site
oligos.notes: 384 distinct barcodes (Plates 1-4, A01-H12). Example A01_Plate1: CAGCACGGCGAGACTGGACGCCTAAGACTTGTC (barcode GGACGCCTAA).

oligos.name: 3LV2_P5_PCR (96 indexed primers, round-3 i5)
oligos.role: Indexed P5 PCR primer; anneals to the N5-constant priming site (CACCGCACGAGAGGT) and adds the i5 index plus the P5 flow-cell adapter

oligos.name: 3LV2_P7_PCR (96 indexed primers, round-3 i7)
oligos.role: Indexed P7 PCR primer; anneals to the N7-constant priming site (CAGCACGGCGAGACT) and adds the i7 index plus the P7 flow-cell adapter

oligos.name: 3Lv2_R1_seq (custom Read 1 primer)
oligos.role: Custom Read 1 sequencing primer; anneals within the ME-A adapter and reads the genomic insert

oligos.name: 3Lv2_R2_seq (custom Read 2 primer)
oligos.role: Custom Read 2 sequencing primer; anneals within the ME-B adapter and reads the genomic insert from the other end

oligos.name: 3Lv2_IDX1 (custom Index 1 primer)
oligos.role: Custom Index 1 (i7 side) sequencing primer; reads the N7 barcode (10 cycles), skips the 15 nt N7 constant (dark), then reads the i7 PCR index (10 cycles)
oligos.notes: Anneals in the ME-B/N7 region; recipe index 1 = 10 cycles + 15 dark cycles + 10 cycles.

oligos.name: 3Lv2_IDX2 (custom Index 2 primer)
oligos.role: Custom Index 2 (i5 side) sequencing primer; reads the N5 barcode (10 cycles), skips the 15 nt N5 constant (dark), then reads the i5 PCR index (10 cycles)
oligos.notes: Anneals in the ME-A/N5 region; recipe index 2 = 10 cycles + 15 dark cycles + 10 cycles.

final_library.source_label: sci-ATAC-seq3 final library (Illumina P5→P7 top strand)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:10](i5) CACCGCACGAGAGGT [CELL_BARCODE:10](N5) GTAATCAG TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG(ME-A) [CDNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC(ME-B rc) GACAAGTC [CELL_BARCODE:10](N7 rc) AGTCTCGCCGTGCTG [SAMPLE_INDEX:10](i7 rc) ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: CACCGCACGAGAGGT                        N5 constant / P5-PCR priming site
GTAATCAG                               N5 linker (ligation junction to ME-A)
TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG      Tn5 mosaic-end A (Read 1 primer site)
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC     Tn5 mosaic-end B (Read 2 site), reverse complement
AGTCTCGCCGTGCTG                        N7 constant / P7-PCR priming site, reverse complement
[SAMPLE_INDEX:10]                      i7 PCR index (round-3 barcode), reverse complement
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG               P7 flow-cell adapter, reverse complement

evidence.locator: sci-ATAC-seq3 final library (Illumina P5→P7 top strand)

library_generation.title: Bulk tagmentation (standard Nextera Tn5)
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are tagmented in bulk with uniformly loaded, non-barcoded Nextera Tn5, inserting mosaic-end adapters into accessible chromatin.
library_generation.note: 200,000 nuclei per sample are tagmented with 2.5 uL Nextera v2 (TDE1) at 55C for 30 min in Nextera TD buffer with digitonin/Tween-20, then stopped with EDTA/spermidine. Because Tn5 acts as a dimer, fragment ends can be ME-A/ME-A, ME-A/ME-B, or ME-B/ME-B; only the A...B species amplifies to a sequenceable library.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [genomic DNA] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC -3'
3'- AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC [genomic DNA] GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG -5'

library_generation.title: 5' phosphorylation (T4 PNK)
library_generation.summary: Tagmented fragment ends are phosphorylated by T4 polynucleotide kinase to prime them for ligation.
library_generation.note: Pooled tagmented nuclei are treated with T4 PNK + 1 mM rATP at 37C for 30 min, adding a 5' phosphate to the mosaic-end adapters so the barcode oligos can be ligated on.

library_generation.title: Round 1 ligation — N5 barcode (split-pool)
library_generation.summary: A splint bridges each ME-A end to a well-specific N5 barcode oligo, which is ligated on as the first combinatorial index.
library_generation.note: Nuclei are split across 384 wells; T7 DNA ligase seals the N5 oligo 3'-OH (...GTAATCAG) to the ME-A 5'-phosphate, guided by the N5 splint (GCCGACGACTGATTA/3ddC). Wells are pooled after ligation.
library_generation.product:
N5 oligo                                ME-A (tagmented fragment)
5'- CACCGCACGAGAGGT[CELL_BARCODE:10]GTAATCAG TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [genomic]...
                        3'-Cdd-ATTAGTCAGCAGCCG-5'   (N5 splint holds ends together)

after ligation:
5'- CACCGCACGAGAGGT[CELL_BARCODE:10]GTAATCAGTCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [genomic]...

library_generation.title: Round 2 ligation — N7 barcode (split-pool)
library_generation.summary: A second splint bridges each ME-B end to a well-specific N7 barcode oligo, ligated on as the second combinatorial index.
library_generation.note: Nuclei are re-split across 384 wells; T7 DNA ligase seals the N7 oligo 3'-OH (...GACTTGTC) to the ME-B 5'-phosphate on the opposite strand, guided by the N7 splint (CACGAGACGACAAGT/3ddC). The first and second ligations can also be performed simultaneously for a 2-level design.
library_generation.product:
5'- CACCGCACGAGAGGT[N5]GTAATCAGTCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG [genomic] CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGACGACAAGTC[N7rc]AGTCTCGCCGTGCTG -3'
3'- GTGGCGTGCTCTCCA[N5rc]CATTAGTCAGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC [genomic] GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG...GACTTGTC[N7]CAGCACGGCGAGACT -5'
(N5 barcode on the ME-A end; N7 barcode on the ME-B end)

library_generation.title: Reverse cross-linking
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are diluted to ~100-300 per uL across plates and reverse-crosslinked to release barcoded fragments.
library_generation.note: EB buffer + Proteinase K + 1% SDS, 65C for 16 h. 1,000-3,000 nuclei per well define the third-level (PCR) compartments.

library_generation.title: Round 3 indexed PCR (P5 + P7)
library_generation.summary: Indexed P5 and P7 primers amplify each well, adding the i5/i7 third-level barcodes and the P5/P7 flow-cell adapters.
library_generation.note: NEBNext/NPM PCR with well-specific 3LV2_P5_PCR (anneals to CACCGCACGAGAGGT) and 3LV2_P7_PCR (anneals to CAGCACGGCGAGACT), 11-13 cycles (cycle number set by qPCR test). Products are cleaned with Zymo DCC-5 then 1X AMPure and quantified on TapeStation.

library_sequencing.note: Custom Read 1 primer identical to the ME-A adapter; anneals across the ME-A mosaic end and reads the accessible-chromatin insert.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 — 3Lv2_R1_seq (custom R1 primer), reads genomic insert from the ME-A end
              TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------->
5'-...GTAATCAGTCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[genomic insert]...-3'
3'-...CATTAGTCAGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[genomic insert]...-5'

library_sequencing.note: Anneals in the ME-B/N7 region and is extended into the N7 (round-2) barcode, then across the 15 nt N7 constant as dark cycles, then into the i7 PCR index. Recipe: 10 + 15 dark + 10 (20 informative bp). Barcodes are read as the reverse complement on this strand.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 1 — 3Lv2_IDX1; reads N7 barcode (10) then i7 index (10), 15 dark cycles between (recipe 10+15dark+10)
      CTCCGAGCCCACGAGACGACAAGTC------->  [N7 bc] | 15 dark (AGTCTCGCCGTGCTG) | [i7]
5'-...CTCCGAGCCCACGAGACGACAAGTC[N7 bc:10]AGTCTCGCCGTGCTG[i7:10]ATCTCG...-3'
3'-...GAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTGCTGTTCAG[N7 bc:10]TCAGAGCGGCACGAC[i7:10]TAGAGC...-5'

library_sequencing.note: Anneals at the N5-linker/ME-A junction on the top strand and is extended leftward into the N5 (round-1) barcode, then across the 15 nt N5 constant as dark cycles, then into the i5 PCR index. Recipe: 10 + 15 dark + 10 (20 informative bp). Barcodes are read as the reverse complement on this strand.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 2 — 3Lv2_IDX2; reads N5 barcode (10) then i5 index (10), 15 dark cycles between (recipe 10+15dark+10)
5'-...CACCGCACGAGAGGT[N5 bc:10]GTAATCAGTCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGT...-3'
3'-...GTGGCGTGCTCTCCA[N5 bc:10]CATTAGTCAGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACA...-5'
            <-------CATTAGTCAGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACA
   [i5] | 15 dark (CACCGCACGAGAGGT) | [N5 bc]

library_sequencing.note: Custom Read 2 primer identical to the ME-B adapter; anneals across the ME-B mosaic end and reads the accessible-chromatin insert from the opposite end.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 — 3Lv2_R2_seq (custom R2 primer), reads genomic insert from the ME-B end
5'-...[genomic insert]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC...-3'
3'-...[genomic insert]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG...-5'
              <-------GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG

title: sci-ATAC-seq3
description: sci-ATAC-seq3 is a three-level single-cell combinatorial-indexing ATAC-seq assay that profiles chromatin accessibility in ~10^6 nuclei without custom barcoded Tn5. Nuclei are tagmented in bulk with standard, uniformly loaded Nextera Tn5, then two successive rounds of in-nucleus splint-mediated ligation append an N5 barcode and an N7 barcode to the two mosaic-end (ME) adapters; a final indexed PCR adds i5/i7 barcodes and the P5/P7 flow-cell sequences. Paired reads (R1/R2) capture the accessible genomic fragment from each ME end, while the two index reads recover the N7+i7 and N5+i5 barcodes that jointly identify each cell. The 384×384×PCR combinatorial scheme (56,623,104 combinations) enables profiling on the order of a million cells per experiment.

reference.label: A human cell atlas of fetal chromatin accessibility

throughput.summary: sci-ATAC-seq3 applied to 59 fetal samples across 15 organs; ~1.6 million cells profiled, 1,568,018 cells identified by barcode; 790,957 high-quality single-cell chromatin accessibility profiles retained from 53 samples. ~110 billion reads (55 billion pairs) across five NovaSeq 6000 runs.
throughput.rna: Not applicable (ATAC-seq / chromatin accessibility assay; companion sci-RNA-seq3 profiled ~4 million cells)
throughput.dna: Median 6,042 nonduplicate fragments per cell (median 5,742 nonduplicate reads/cell); estimated collision rate 1-4%

publication.statistical_model: Mixture of two negative binomials to separate cell barcodes from background; negative binomial regression for TF-motif enrichment across cell types

other.label: Master peak set
other.value: 1.05 million accessible sites spanning 532 Mb (17.1% of the human genome)

other.label: Protocol / data
other.value: protocols.io 10.17504/protocols.io.be8mjhu6; demultiplexing on Zenodo (10.5281/zenodo.4012524); data at GEO GSE149683 / dbGaP phs002003.v1.p1

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by their combinatorial N5/N7/i5/i7 barcodes to assign each read to a single cell, deduplicated, and used to call accessibility peaks (MACS2) merged into a 1.05M-site master set; real cell barcodes are separated from background by fitting a mixture of two negative binomials, then cells are QC- and doublet-filtered (scrublet), reduced by log-scaled LSI (TF-IDF + SVD), batch-corrected (Harmony), Louvain-clustered and UMAP-embedded (Seurat V3), and annotated by NNLS label-transfer from the companion sci-RNA-seq atlas, followed by JASPAR/homer motif enrichment, Cicero co-accessibility, LDSC heritability, and QuASAR allelic-imbalance analyses.
data_processing.statistical_model: mixture of two negative binomials (cell calling); negative binomial regression (motif enrichment and pseudotime-dependent accessibility)
